title: Orange alert for heatwave issued in five districts of Rajasthan - News On AIR
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
full_text:
Amid the continuing spell of extreme heat in Rajasthan, an orange alert for heatwave conditions has been issued today in five districts of the state. Maximum temperatures are expected to cross 46 degrees Celsius in parts of the Jodhpur, Bikaner and Kota divisions, as well as the Shekhawati region.
Most parts of Rajasthan continue to reel under intense heatwave conditions. People are facing severe discomfort during the day, while nights remain unusually warm, offering little relief. Minimum temperatures above 30 degrees Celsius have been recorded in several cities, including Jaipur, Kota, Jodhpur, Nagaur, Bikaner and Dausa. Kota recorded the warmest night in the state, with the minimum temperature hovering around 33 degrees Celsius.
In view of the severe heatwave and hot winds, the administration has intensified relief measures. These include sprinkling water on roads, setting up temporary shelters, and ensuring the availability of drinking water at various locations.

title: Rain alert from May 28, mercury touches 47°C: IMD issues heatwave warning for 5 Rajasthan districts; Sri Ga... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Rain alert from May 28, mercury touches 47°C:IMD issues heatwave warning for 5 Rajasthan districts; Sri Ganganagar remains hottest
Rajasthan continues to reel under intense heatwave conditions during the ongoing Nautapa period, with temperatures soaring across several districts. On Tuesday, Sri Ganganagar recorded the highest temperature in the state at 47 degrees Celsius, while Bikaner, Jaisalmer, Phalodi, and Kota remained in the grip of severe heatwave conditions.
However, signs of weather change have started emerging in some areas. Sikar witnessed cloudy skies, strong winds, and light rainfall on Tuesday evening. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for heatwave conditions in five districts on May 27, while a yellow alert for rain and thunderstorms has been issued from May 28 to 30.
Severe heatwave sweeps across Rajasthan
Northwestern districts of Rajasthan experienced extreme heat over the last 24 hours. Sri Ganganagar remained the hottest city at 47°C.

Since Tuesday morning, scorching sunshine and strong hot winds intensified heatwave conditions across these regions.
Nights remain uncomfortably warm
Several cities are also witnessing unusually high night temperatures, offering little relief from the daytime heat.

Kota recorded the warmest night in the state with a minimum temperature of 32.8°C.
